name: QualBench-v0
description: 10 real-world issues measuring AI code production readiness

issues.title: Missing error handler for HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed
issues.problem_statement: When a route exists but the HTTP method is not allowed, Flask returns a generic 405 response without invoking any registered error handler. Users who register `@app.errorhandler(405)` expect their custom handler to be called, but it is bypassed when the MethodNotAllowed exception is raised by the routing layer. Expected: custom 405 handler is invoked. Actual: default werkzeug 405 response is returned.
issues.expected_fix: Ensure MethodNotAllowed exceptions from the router pass through the app's error handler dispatch.

issues.title: Incorrect encoding detection for responses with BOM
issues.problem_statement: When a server returns a response with a UTF-8 BOM (byte order mark) but does not declare charset in Content-Type header, requests falls back to apparent_encoding (chardet/charset_normalizer) which sometimes detects the encoding incorrectly as ascii or latin-1. The BOM should take precedence as an explicit encoding signal. Expected: UTF-8 BOM detected, response.text decoded as UTF-8. Actual: response.text decoded as latin-1, producing garbled text.
issues.expected_fix: Check for BOM bytes at the start of response content before falling back to apparent_encoding.

issues.title: Missing validation for negative values in integer query parameter with ge=0
issues.problem_statement: When declaring a query parameter with `Query(ge=0)`, FastAPI should reject negative integer values with a 422 Validation Error. However, when the query parameter is passed as a string like `?page=-1`, the validation passes and the negative value is accepted. This only happens when the parameter type annotation uses `int` with `Query(ge=0)` — using `conint(ge=0)` works correctly. Expected: `GET /items?page=-1` returns 422. Actual: returns 200 with page=-1.
issues.expected_fix: Ensure Query parameter validators apply ge/le/gt/lt constraints consistently regardless of declaration style.

issues.title: Template filter |escape inconsistency with mark_safe in nested blocks
issues.problem_statement: When using the `|escape` filter on a variable that was previously marked safe with `mark_safe()`, the filter should re-escape the content. However, inside nested `{% block %}` tags with template inheritance, the `|escape` filter is silently ignored for mark_safe'd content. This creates an inconsistency: the same expression `{{ var|escape }}` produces different results depending on whether it appears in a top-level template or inside an inherited block. Expected: `|escape` always escapes, regardless of block nesting depth. Actual: `|escape` is no-op inside nested inherited blocks for mark_safe content.
issues.expected_fix: Fix the template engine's escape filter to consistently apply escaping regardless of block inheritance context.

issues.title: Connection pool timeout not respected when all connections are in use
issues.problem_statement: When using `httpx.AsyncClient` with `limits=httpx.Limits(max_connections=10)` and all 10 connections are in use, new requests should wait up to `pool_timeout` seconds for a connection to become available, then raise `PoolTimeout`. Instead, requests wait indefinitely — the pool_timeout parameter is ignored when the pool is full. Expected: `PoolTimeout` raised after `pool_timeout` seconds. Actual: request hangs forever.
issues.expected_fix: Implement timeout enforcement in the connection pool acquisition path.

issues.title: Recursive model serialization exceeds max depth without clear error
issues.problem_statement: When a Pydantic model has a self-referential field (e.g., `children: list['TreeNode']`), calling `.model_dump()` on a deeply nested instance causes a `RecursionError` with no indication of what went wrong or how to fix it. Expected: clear error message with the model name, field path, and current depth — or a configurable `max_depth` parameter on `model_dump()`. Actual: raw `RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded`.
issues.expected_fix: Add max_depth parameter to model_dump() and model_dump_json() with a clear error when exceeded.

issues.title: Extract blueprint error handling logic into a dedicated module
issues.problem_statement: Flask's `app.py` contains error handling logic that is tightly coupled with blueprint registration, making it difficult to customize error handling per-blueprint. The error handling code (dispatching errors to handlers, looking up blueprint-specific handlers, falling back to app-level handlers) is spread across `Flask.handle_exception`, `Flask.handle_user_exception`, and parts of `Flask.full_dispatch_request`. Refactor: extract all error handling logic into a new `flask/error_handling.py` module with a clear `ErrorDispatcher` class. Preserve all existing behavior. No API changes.
issues.expected_fix: New module `flask/error_handling.py` with `ErrorDispatcher` class. All existing error handling tests must pass without modification.

issues.title: Reduce middleware chain cyclomatic complexity
issues.problem_statement: Starlette's middleware stack in `starlette/middleware/__init__.py` and the ASGI app wrapping logic in `starlette/applications.py` has grown to CC > 15 in the `build_middleware_stack` method. The nested conditionals handle different middleware types (pure ASGI, class-based, function-based) and error recovery. Refactor to bring CC below 10 while preserving all behavior. Key constraint: no changes to the public Middleware API.
issues.expected_fix: Refactor build_middleware_stack to use strategy pattern or similar. CC must drop below 10. All middleware tests pass.

issues.title: Path parameter injection in generated OpenAPI schema URL
issues.problem_statement: FastAPI's OpenAPI schema generation interpolates path parameters into example URLs without sanitization. A malicious path parameter value containing JavaScript or HTML (e.g., `<script>alert(1)</script>`) is rendered unsanitized in the Swagger UI example panel. This is a stored XSS via OpenAPI spec. Severity: Medium (requires crafted parameter value displayed in docs). Expected: all path parameter examples are HTML-escaped in the generated OpenAPI JSON. Actual: raw HTML/JS rendered in Swagger UI.
issues.expected_fix: Escape path parameter examples in OpenAPI schema generation. Add Content-Security-Policy headers to docs endpoints.

issues.title: Reflected XSS in admin changelist filter parameter
issues.problem_statement: The Django admin changelist view reflects filter parameter names in the page HTML without proper escaping. A crafted URL like `/admin/app/model/?<script>alert(1)</script>=value` renders the script tag in the filter sidebar. This only affects authenticated admin users but can be exploited via CSRF or social engineering. Severity: Medium. Expected: all filter parameter names are escaped before rendering. Actual: raw HTML in filter sidebar.
issues.expected_fix: Escape filter parameter names in the admin changelist template and/or view. Add test for XSS in filter params.
